title: strychnos mitscherlichii var. mitscherlichii
intro: Strychnos mitscherlichii var. mitscherlichii, also known as the Chacha Tree, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Loganiaceae family. The plant's bark and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Strychnos mitscherlichii var. mitscherlichii include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential antiparasitic and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its bark and roots, which can be dried, powdered, and then consumed as a tea or capsule.
